chapter_context.summary: Responds to the prayer by declaring judgment on persistent idolaters, promising inheritance for His servants, and announcing the creation of new heavens and earth.

outline.explanation: This section focuses on God's Response: Calling Rebellious Idolaters (65:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Judgment on Idolaters vs. Blessings for Servants
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Judgment on Idolaters vs. Blessings for Servants (65:8-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Creation of New Heavens and New Earth; Peace in Nature
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Creation of New Heavens and New Earth; Peace in Nature (65:17-25), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Glory and Future Hope
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of glory and future hope, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 65, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
